title: Detty December Festival 2026
description: The ultimate Lagos experience — 11 days of non-stop concerts, beach parties, cultural showcases, and celebrity appearances across multiple venues.
long_description:
Detty December Festival is the flagship event of Lagos's legendary December season. Spanning 11 days from December 20 to New Year's Eve, this multi-venue festival brings together the biggest names in Afrobeats, international guest artists, and emerging talent.

Expect massive concert stages, curated food villages featuring Lagos's best restaurants, fashion pop-ups from Nigerian designers, art installations, and VIP experiences. The festival culminates in one of the most spectacular New Year's Eve celebrations on the African continent.

Previous editions have featured Wizkid, Davido, Burna Boy, Tiwa Savage, Asake, and surprise international guests. Multiple ticket tiers are available, from General Admission to exclusive VIP and VVIP packages.

title: Felabration 2026
description: A week-long celebration of the life and legacy of Fela Anikulapo Kuti — the father of Afrobeat. Live music, panel discussions, art exhibitions, and dance at the New Afrika Shrine.
long_description:
Felabration is one of Africa's most important music festivals — a week-long tribute to Fela Anikulapo Kuti held annually at the New Afrika Shrine in Ikeja, the venue built to continue Fela's legacy.

The festival features live performances from top Afrobeat, highlife, juju, and contemporary Nigerian musicians. Femi and Seun Kuti (Fela's sons) typically headline alongside a diverse lineup of Nigerian and international artists.

Beyond music, Felabration includes panel discussions on Fela's political legacy, art exhibitions, dance competitions (including the famous Fela dance-off), and symposiums on African culture and resistance.

The atmosphere is electric, raw, and deeply political — exactly as Fela would have wanted it. This is not a sanitized tourist event; it's a genuine cultural happening that celebrates art as a tool for social change.

Dress code: Be yourself. The Fela spirit is about authenticity and freedom of expression.

title: Osun-Osogbo Festival 2026
description: A two-week UNESCO-recognized festival honouring the Yoruba goddess Osun at her sacred grove. Hundreds of thousands gather for rituals, processions, and the iconic Arugba ceremony.
long_description:
The Osun-Osogbo Festival is the most important annual celebration of Yoruba spirituality — a two-week event that culminates in the grand procession to the Osun River within the UNESCO World Heritage Sacred Grove.

The festival centres on the Arugba — a chosen virgin priestess who carries the sacred calabash (igbadu) on her head from the Ataoja's Palace to the grove. Thousands line the streets to watch her procession, accompanied by traditional drummers, hunters, masquerades (Egungun), and devotees.

Throughout the festival period, various rituals and ceremonies take place: the Iwopopo (cleaning of the grove path), the Iboriade (crowning ritual), and the Ede gathering. The atmosphere blends deep spiritual reverence with carnival-like celebration.

The festival draws devotees from across the Yoruba diaspora — practitioners of Candomblé from Brazil, Santería from Cuba, and Vodou from Haiti all trace their spiritual roots to this very grove and goddess.

Visitors should dress modestly, follow guides' instructions at the grove, and be prepared for large crowds. This is primarily a religious festival, not a tourist show.

title: Art X Lagos 2026
description: West Africa's premier international art fair — showcasing contemporary African art from galleries across the continent and diaspora. Exhibitions, talks, and performances.
long_description:
Art X Lagos is West Africa's premier international art fair, bringing together leading galleries from across Africa and the diaspora to showcase contemporary art in a stunning waterfront setting.

The fair features curated exhibitions, solo presentations, panel discussions with leading artists and curators, live performances, and installations. Expect groundbreaking work from established names and exciting discoveries from emerging African artists.

The event draws collectors, curators, and art enthusiasts from around the world and has become a key date on the global art calendar. It's a powerful demonstration of Lagos's growing status as a world-class art capital.

title: Eyo Festival 2026
description: Lagos's most iconic cultural event — white-robed Eyo masquerades parade through Lagos Island in a ceremony honouring departed dignitaries. A spectacle unlike anything else in Africa.
long_description:
The Eyo Festival (Adamu Orisha Play) is one of the most visually stunning cultural events in Africa. Hundreds of masquerades (Eyo) dressed entirely in white — flowing agbada robes, white hats (akeleje), and carrying long staffs (opambata) — parade through the streets of Lagos Island.

The festival is held to honour departed royalty, chiefs, or prominent Lagosians. Its exact dates are determined by the Oba of Lagos and traditional rulers, making each staging a rare and special occasion.

Eyo masquerades represent the spirits of the dead and are treated with deep reverence. Five groups (Eyo Adimu, Eyo Laba, Eyo Olokun, Eyo Agere, and Eyo Oniko) each emerge from their traditional compounds and converge at Tafawa Balewa Square.

The procession is accompanied by traditional drumming, dancing, and the distinctive 'crack' of the Eyo staffs. The entire city of Lagos Island effectively shuts down for this event.

This is Lagos at its most traditional and magnificent — a living ceremony that predates colonial rule and continues to assert the cultural identity of the city's indigenous Awori and Bini communities.

title: Aké Arts & Book Festival 2026
description: Nigeria's foremost literary and arts festival — celebrating African literature, visual arts, and music in the historic city of Abeokuta, birthplace of literary giants.
long_description:
The Aké Arts & Book Festival celebrates the rich literary tradition of Nigeria and Africa. Named after 'Aké,' Wole Soyinka's memoir of his childhood in Abeokuta, this festival brings together writers, poets, artists, and thinkers from across the continent and diaspora.

The programme includes readings, panel discussions, book launches, poetry performances, art exhibitions, and music. Past speakers have included Chimamanda Ngozi Adichie, Wole Soyinka, and other leading voices in African literature.

Held in Abeokuta — the birthplace of Soyinka, Funmilayo Ransome-Kuti, and other Nigerian intellectual giants — the festival celebrates a city with an outsized influence on Nigerian culture and thought.

The intimate scale of the festival allows for genuine interaction with authors and artists, making it one of the most rewarding cultural events in West Africa.

title: Afrobeats December Concert Series
description: Lagos's biggest concert season — Wizkid Live, Davido's TTWD, Burna Boy's African Giant concert, and more. The headliners that make Detty December legendary.
long_description:
December in Lagos means one thing: concerts. The biggest names in Afrobeats — Wizkid, Davido, Burna Boy, Asake, Rema, Tiwa Savage, and more — all headline massive shows during the last two weeks of December.

Venues like Eko Convention Centre, Tafawa Balewa Square, and Eko Atlantic host thousands of revellers nightly. Each artist typically stages their own branded event — Wizkid's 'Made in Lagos' concert, Davido's 'Timeless Tour,' Burna Boy's 'African Giant' show.

Beyond the headliners, dozens of smaller concerts, rooftop parties, and beach raves fill every night. The energy is absolutely electric — this is when Lagos truly earns its reputation as Africa's entertainment capital.

Tickets for headliner shows sell out fast, especially VIP and VVIP tiers. Early booking is essential.

title: Lagos Food & Drink Festival 2026
description: A celebration of Nigerian cuisine — from suya and jollof rice to fine dining experiments. Chef demos, tastings, street food markets, and cocktail masterclasses.
long_description:
The Lagos Food & Drink Festival celebrates Nigeria's extraordinary culinary diversity. From the smoky perfection of northern suya to the heated debates over jollof rice supremacy, Nigerian food is having a global moment — and this festival showcases it all.

Expect live cooking demonstrations from Nigeria's top chefs, street food markets featuring vendors from across the country, cocktail masterclasses using local spirits and ingredients, and panel discussions on the future of Nigerian gastronomy.

The festival is held across multiple venues in Victoria Island and Lekki, with both daytime market events and evening dining experiences. It's a perfect pre-Detty December warm-up for visitors arriving early in the month.
